meta.title: GCSE Maths: Foundation & Higher (9-1), AQA, Edexcel, OCR
meta.description: GCSE Maths course for AQA, Edexcel and OCR: Foundation and Higher tiers, non-calculator Paper 1 fluency, grade 7-9 reach and past-paper technique.
meta.level: Years 9-11 GCSE students, Foundation and Higher tiers, plus resit candidates
meta.commitment: 2 live one-hour classes per week + weekly past-paper work
meta.certification: Course completion certificate + documented paper-score progression

program_overview.title: GCSE Mathematics Mastery
program_overview.tagline: Foundation to grade 5 secure, Higher to grade 9, taught for understanding, trained for the papers.
program_overview.description: An 18-month live program for GCSE mathematics across AQA, Edexcel and OCR, at Foundation or Higher tier. GCSE maths is three papers, one strictly non-calculator, with grades that gatekeep sixth form, apprenticeships and A-Level maths. This course teaches the syllabus understanding-first (so the mock slide of year 11 never happens), trains bare-hands fluency for Paper 1 in every class, brings the Higher-tier grade 7-9 topics in early enough to settle, and converts it all into marks through past-paper cycles against your exact board's mark schemes.
what_makes_this_different: Board-exact: AQA, Edexcel or OCR confirmed first, and every past paper comes from your board
Tier-honest: evidence for Foundation-vs-Higher decisions, and a real runway from grade 4-risk to 6-secure, or 6 to 9
Non-calculator Paper 1 fluency drilled in every class
The grade 7-9 separator topics taught months early so they settle instead of being crammed
Mark-scheme literacy: method marks, working discipline and the plus-minus habits that stop point leaks
Understanding-first teaching: algebra as a language, so re-framed exam questions do not break the student
One dedicated mentor across the whole GCSE run, a full interactive hour, twice a week
Resit candidates welcome, with a compressed evidence-led plan

learning_path.phase_1: The Foundation core built properly: number fluency, algebra as a language, and the geometry and data basics
learning_path.phase_2: The Higher reach with settling time: quadratics, functions, trigonometry, vectors and the grade 7-9 separator topics
learning_path.phase_3: Past-paper craft by board: all three papers, timing plans, mark banking and full cycles into the exam series

career_outcomes: A GCSE grade that opens sixth-form, apprenticeship and A-Level doors
Higher-tier command that makes A-Level maths a step, not a cliff
The numeracy confidence every future pathway quietly requires
Exam craft that transfers to every exam the student will ever sit

phase_1_foundation.title: PHASE 1: The Core, Built Properly (Months 1-6)
phase_1_foundation.description: Foundation-tier territory owned deeply is what Higher-tier grades stand on. This phase rebuilds number fluency without a calculator, teaches algebra as a language, and secures the geometry and statistics basics, with the diagnostic finding the true starting point first.

week_1_2.title: Diagnostic, Board and Tier Fit
week_1_2.description: Full diagnostic: board confirmed, tier fit assessed honestly, non-calculator fluency and algebra base measured
topics: Exam board confirmed: AQA, Edexcel or OCR
Foundation versus Higher tier fit assessed honestly
week_1_2.practice: A mixed non-calculator and algebra diagnostic set sized to the suspected tier.
week_1_2.assessment: Baseline grade estimate with a written tier recommendation and a topic-priority map.

week_3_4.title: Fractions, Decimals and Percentages to Fluency
week_3_4.description: Fractions, decimals and percentages to fluency, Paper 1's exact territory, with estimation as a checking habit
topics: Percentage of, increase, decrease and reverse
Estimation and rounding as a check
week_3_4.practice: A non-calculator FDP set with estimation checks on every answer.

week_5_6.title: Ratio and Proportion Reasoning
week_5_6.description: Ratio and proportion reasoning: the best-buy, recipe and map-scale families
week_5_6.practice: Ratio and proportion questions across the best-buy, recipe and map-scale families.

week_7_8.title: Indices, Standard Form and Bounds
week_7_8.description: Indices, standard form and bounds; error intervals done with meaning
topics: Index laws with integer and negative powers
week_7_8.practice: An indices, standard-form and bounds set including error-interval questions.

week_9_10.title: Expressions, Expansion and Factorisation
week_9_10.description: Expressions, expansion and factorisation with the area model underneath
week_9_10.practice: An expand-and-factorise set anchored by area-model diagrams.

week_11_12.title: Linear Equations, Inequalities and Formulae
week_11_12.description: Linear equations and inequalities read as sentences; rearranging formulae
topics: Solving linear equations step by step
Rearranging formulae to change the subject
Reading an equation as a sentence
week_11_12.practice: Linear equation, inequality and rearrangement questions with worded contexts.

week_13_14.title: Straight-Line Graphs and y = mx + c
week_13_14.description: Straight-line graphs: gradient as rate, y = mx + c as a story; real-life graphs read critically
topics: y = mx + c as intercept and slope
Finding the equation of a line
week_13_14.practice: Straight-line graph questions plus interpretation of a real-life rate graph.

week_15_16.title: Simultaneous Equations and Sequences
week_15_16.description: Simultaneous equations by structure; sequences and the nth term
topics: Linear sequences and the nth term
Finding and using the nth term rule
week_15_16.practice: A simultaneous-equations and nth-term set graded by structure.

week_17_18.title: Angle Reasoning and Justification
week_17_18.description: Angle reasoning with justification language: parallel lines, polygons, bearings
topics: Angles on lines and around points
Interior and exterior angles of polygons
Stating a reason for every step
week_17_18.practice: Angle problems requiring named reasons, including bearings.

week_19_20.title: Area, Perimeter, Volume and Pythagoras
week_19_20.description: Area, perimeter and volume including compound shapes; Pythagoras from a picture
topics: Area and perimeter of standard shapes
Volume and surface area of solids
Units for length, area and volume
week_19_20.practice: A mensuration set combining compound shapes and Pythagoras problems.

week_21_22.title: Averages, Charts and Correlation
week_21_22.description: Averages, charts, scatter graphs and correlation, data read like a sceptic
topics: Bar charts, pie charts and pictograms
Scatter graphs and lines of best fit
week_21_22.practice: A statistics set interpreting charts and a scatter graph with commentary.

week_23_24.title: Phase 1 Checkpoint Paper
week_23_24.description: Phase checkpoint: a full Foundation-standard paper under timing, reviewed against the mark scheme
topics: Error log started from the checkpoint
week_23_24.practice: A complete Foundation-standard paper sat to time, then self-marked.
week_23_24.assessment: Foundation-standard checkpoint paper marked against the real mark scheme, opening the error log.

phase_2_higher.title: PHASE 2: The Higher Reach, With Settling Time (Months 7-12)
phase_2_higher.description: The topics that separate grade 5 from grade 9, taught months before the exam so they are owned rather than crammed: quadratics and functions, trigonometry beyond right angles, vectors, circle theorems and the algebraic craft the top questions assume.

week_25_26.title: Solving Quadratics Three Ways
week_25_26.description: Quadratics by factorising, completing the square and the formula, chosen by structure
week_25_26.practice: A quadratics set requiring choice of factorising, completing the square or the formula.

week_27_28.title: Quadratic Graphs and Turning Points
week_27_28.description: Quadratic graphs, turning points and real contexts; quadratic inequalities (Higher)
topics: Turning points by completing the square
week_27_28.practice: Quadratic-graph questions including a Higher-tier quadratic inequality.

week_29_30.title: Functions: Composite and Inverse
week_29_30.description: Function notation, composite and inverse functions, where half the function marks are lost
topics: Inverse functions and how to find them
week_29_30.practice: A functions set focused on composite and inverse questions.

week_31_32.title: Graph Transformations and Algebraic Fractions
week_31_32.description: Graph transformations, cubic and reciprocal graphs; algebraic fractions (Higher)
week_31_32.practice: Graph-transformation questions plus a Higher-tier algebraic-fractions set.

month_9_10.title: Months 9-10: Trigonometry, Vectors and Circle Theorems

week_33_34.title: Right-Triangle Trigonometry and Exact Values
week_33_34.description: Right-triangle trig consolidated; exact trig values for Paper 1
topics: Finding sides and angles in right triangles
week_33_34.practice: Right-triangle trig questions including exact-value non-calculator items.

week_35_36.title: Sine and Cosine Rules
week_35_36.description: Sine and cosine rules with bearings and area applications; trig graphs (Higher)
topics: The sine rule for sides and angles
The cosine rule for sides and angles
Area of a triangle with the sine formula
week_35_36.practice: A non-right-triangle set applying sine and cosine rules with bearings.

week_37_38.title: Vectors and Geometric Proof
week_37_38.description: Vectors and the classic geometric vector proof, defanged (Higher)
week_37_38.practice: Vector questions building to a full geometric vector proof.

week_39_40.title: Circle Theorems
topics: Angle at the centre and at the circumference
week_39_40.practice: Circle-theorem problems requiring a named theorem for each step.

month_11_12.title: Months 11-12: Probability, Proportion and the Grade 8-9 Craft

week_41_42.title: Probability, Trees and Venn Diagrams
week_41_42.description: Probability with tree and Venn diagrams; conditional probability without formula-panic
topics: Probability of single and combined events
week_41_42.practice: A probability set using tree and Venn diagrams, including a conditional question.

week_43_44.title: Proportion and Rates of Change
week_43_44.description: Direct and inverse proportion as equations; rates of change and pre-calculus gradient ideas (Higher)
topics: Gradient of a curve by tangent (Higher)
week_43_44.practice: Proportion questions written as equations plus a rate-of-change graph task.

week_45_46.title: Histograms and Cumulative Frequency
week_45_46.description: Histograms, cumulative frequency and comparing distributions like a statistician
week_45_46.practice: A set drawing and reading histograms, cumulative frequency curves and box plots.

week_47_48.title: Phase 2 Higher-Tier Checkpoint
week_47_48.description: Phase checkpoint: full Higher-tier past-paper cycle with mark-scheme review and error-log update
week_47_48.practice: A full Higher-tier past paper sat to time and reviewed against the mark scheme.
week_47_48.assessment: Higher-tier past-paper cycle marked to the mark scheme, updating the error log and weak-topic list.

phase_3_exam_craft.title: PHASE 3: Paper Craft to the Exam Series (Months 13-18)
phase_3_exam_craft.description: Three papers, one strictly non-calculator, sat over exam season: this phase turns understanding into grades with board-exact past-paper cycles, personal timing plans, mark-banking discipline and a peak plan that meets the series calmly.

week_49_52.title: Paper 1 Craft, Non-Calculator
week_49_52.description: Paper 1 craft: non-calculator discipline, exact values and clean written working at speed
topics: Exact values for surds and trig
week_49_52.practice: Timed Paper 1 sections focused on non-calculator accuracy and clean working.

week_53_56.title: Papers 2 and 3 Calculator Craft
week_53_56.description: Papers 2-3 craft: calculator use that helps, multi-step chains, and method-mark harvesting on problems you cannot finish
week_53_56.practice: Timed Paper 2 and 3 sections practising method-mark harvesting on unfinished problems.

week_57_60.title: Full Board-Exact Paper Cycles
week_57_60.description: Full timed papers from your exact board and tier; review rituals, why each mark was lost, and the named fix
topics: Full timed papers from the exact board
A review ritual after every paper
Naming why each mark was lost
A named fix for each error
week_57_60.practice: Complete board-exact papers sat to time, each followed by a why-and-fix review.

week_61_64.title: Timing Plans and Weak-Topic Rotations
week_61_64.description: Timing plans from personal data; weakest-topic rotations by error-log priority; grade-boundary awareness used honestly
topics: Personal timing plans from paper data
week_61_64.practice: Weak-topic rotation sets chosen by error-log priority, timed to the personal plan.

week_65_68.title: Final Cycles and Error-Log Closure
week_65_68.description: Final cycles with documented progression; error-log closure; targeted revision that respects the mocks calendar
topics: Targeted revision to the mocks calendar
week_65_68.practice: Final paper cycles with progression charted and each closed error re-tested.

week_69_72.title: Peak Plan and Exam-Day Routines
week_69_72.description: Peak plan: light consolidations, rest discipline, and exam-day routines rehearsed across all three papers
topics: Light consolidation in the peak week
The series met as a known place
week_69_72.practice: Light consolidation tasks and a rehearsed exam-day routine across the three papers.
week_69_72.assessment: Final full-series rehearsal across all three papers with documented progression before the exam.

faqs.question: Which exam boards does this course cover?
faqs.answer: AQA, Edexcel and OCR, the content overlaps heavily, but paper styles and mark schemes differ, so the first thing the mentor confirms is your school's board, and every past paper comes from it. The 9-1 grading system and three-paper structure (one non-calculator) frame all exam work.

faqs.question: Foundation or Higher, how should the decision be made?
faqs.answer: By destination, then evidence. Foundation caps the grade at 5, so if A-Level maths or a competitive sixth form is even possible, Higher is the default. Whether the gaps blocking Higher are repairable in the time available is a diagnostic question, and the free demo answers it with evidence you can take to school.

faqs.question: My child's grades slide every mock as content deepens. Why?
faqs.answer: That slide is the classic signature of procedure without understanding: memorized methods hold at grade 4-5 content and collapse when questions chain or re-frame. Understanding-first teaching is the fix, algebra as a language, geometry with justification, which is exactly how this course teaches.

faqs.question: Can you take a grade 6 student to grade 8-9?
faqs.answer: That jump is made of specific things: the Higher separator topics (functions, vectors, circle theorems, proportion craft) owned early, Paper 1 fluency, and mark-scheme literacy on the long questions. All are taught directly in Phases 2-3. The honest caveat: the earlier the start, the more settling time the grade 8-9 topics get.

faqs.question: Do you take resit candidates?
faqs.answer: Yes, post-16 resit students get a compressed, evidence-led version of the program prioritised by their diagnostic, aimed at the grade 4/5 threshold or beyond as their goals require.

faqs.question: How does this fit around school mocks and revision?
faqs.answer: The mentor plans against your school calendar: intensity rises before mocks, past-paper focus aligns with what school is testing, and the final months are sequenced around the real series. The course reinforces school rather than competing with it.

faqs.question: What does the course cost?
faqs.answer: Small-group classes are ₹1,499 per month and private 1-on-1 mentorship is ₹4,999 per month in India; international students, including UK families, pay USD $100 per month for group and $150 for 1-on-1, with 8 live one-hour classes a month and recordings included. UK tutors typically charge £25-£50 per hour for the same format.

faqs.question: Can we try it before paying?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every student starts with a free live demo class that doubles as the diagnostic, board, tier, fluency and algebra base, with no card details required.

testimonials.text: My son struggled with math for years. Integrating it into coding projects has transformed his understanding and confidence. Highly recommended!

testimonials.name: Sonam Oswal, mother of Dhairya (verified Google review)
testimonials.text: My child Dhairya is really enjoying the classes. This is his first online class, and he eagerly looks forward to it. I can see his improvement.

course_guarantees.small_batches: Small batches only: group classes are capped at 10 students, with mini-batch (3 to 4 students) and personal 1-on-1 options.
course_guarantees.structured_curriculum: A structured, well-paced curriculum taught step by step, with hands-on practice in every session.
course_guarantees.doubt_support: Doubt support between classes over WhatsApp, so you are never left stuck.
course_guarantees.certificate: A course-completion certificate you can share.
